rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN T. GO VS. DAVID P. CARAVANTES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming Referee'S Report On Offsets
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming Referee'S Report On Offsets is HEARING REQUIRED. This matter was continued from May 16 to May 23, 2025 for the Referee to provide a declaration addressing two concerns the Court had with the Referee's Report filed on March 18, 2025. The first dealt with the attorney's fees awarded to Plaintiff, and second with the HELOC payments. The Court provided Plaintiff with an opportunity to respond to the Referee's subsequent findings. The Court having read and considered the Referee's May 19, 2025 declaration and Plaintiff's counsel's response, accepts the Referee's conclusions. The Referee's Report filed on March 18, 2025 shall be amended to recommend a total award of $85,189.23 for the Plaintiff's attorney's fees. The Referee's recommendation on the HELOC loan stands. The Court considered Plaintiff's counsel's declaration which claims that the Referee's accounting still does not take into account all attorney invoices through August 30, 2024. However, a review of the index, (pages 41-62), shows that the Referee did account for all invoices. While counsel's declaration claims she provided all invoices through August 30, 2024, it would appear that is not case. (Valdepena decl., para. 6.) Notably, the appendixes were filed with the court and counsel could have, as the Court did, reviewed the invoices the Referee used in his initial evaluation. Instead, counsel waited until she filed her supplemental declaration to claim that "invoices were provided but seem to be missing." Moreover, she includes none of the missing invoices to her declaration. The Referee is ordered to file an amended report consistent with his amended findings forthwith. The Court finds the Referee's findings and recommendations are supported by law and/or the evidence submitted by the parties. Counsel for the moving party shall provide an order consistent with this tentative ruling and a separate order granting the motion confirming the Referee's amended report. Defendant's request for order allowing both parties to credit bid is denied as premature. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PERRY-THISTLE VS. AMBER QUELVOG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment (Ccp 872.720)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2025 line 6. PLAINTIFF ELLEN PERRY-THISTLE AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment (Ccp 872.720) is DENIED. The Real Property Partition Act applies to all actions for partition of real property filed on or after January 1, 2023, where the real property is held in tenancy in common and there is no agreement in a record binding all the cotenants that governs the partition of the property. (CCP section 874.311, subds. (b)-(c).) Plaintiff applies for an Interlocutory Judgement under CCP section 872.720. Plaintiff has not provided any authority that the Court may issue an interlocutory judgment at this stage and still comply with the provisions of the Real Property Partition Act. The Court notes that Plaintiff did not even address the Real Property Partition Act on reply. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AMY BEASOM VS. KAIA ANDERSON-BUCKLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2025 line 10. DEFENDANT KAIA ANDERSON-BUCKLEY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT HEARING REQUIRED. No later than 9:00 a.m. on May 23, 2025, defendant to file a proper proof of service by the messenger stating, specifically, how the moving papers for this motion were served on plaintiff. The messenger's proof of service filed with this motion states that the papers were delivered to the plaintiff's address, not to a person. This is insufficient to show whether personal service was properly effectuated. (CCP 1011.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
